datasets.name: claims_by_benefit_param
queryLines:
SELECT
  clm_dtl_benefit_category,
  clm_dtl_place_of_service,
  clm_dtl_specific_dos_date,
  clm_dtl_paid_amt,
  clm_dtl_allowed_amt,
  clm_dtl_claim_id,
  clm_dtl_line_status
FROM
  aira_test.aibi_workshop_schema.fact_claim_detail
WHERE
  (
    COALESCE(ARRAY_SIZE(:benefit_category), 0) = 0
    OR array_contains(:benefit_category, clm_dtl_benefit_category)
  )
  AND clm_dtl_specific_dos_date
    >= COALESCE(
      :service_date_range.min,
      (
        SELECT
          MIN(clm_dtl_specific_dos_date)
        FROM
          aira_test.aibi_workshop_schema.fact_claim_detail
      )
    )
  AND clm_dtl_specific_dos_date
    <= COALESCE(
      :service_date_range.max,
      (
        SELECT
          MAX(clm_dtl_specific_dos_date)
        FROM
          aira_test.aibi_workshop_schema.fact_claim_detail
      )

datasets.name: benefit_category_lookup
queryLines:
SELECT DISTINCT
  clm_dtl_benefit_category
FROM
  aira_test.aibi_workshop_schema.fact_claim_detail
WHERE
  clm_dtl_benefit_category IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  clm_dtl_benefit_category

datasets.name: 228586d4
queryLines: where `Service Date` = coalesce((Select max(`Service Date`) from aira_test.aibi_workshop_schema.mv_claims), :p_default_service_date)

frame.title: Active Members by Line of Business
Paid vs Allowed vs Billed by Year
Avg Cost per Member by LOB

lines:
## Parameter-Based Filtering: Benefit Category Example

This page demonstrates **parameter-based filtering** — a pattern where the filter widget passes selected values directly into a SQL `:parameter` in the dataset query's `WHERE` clause. Unlike column-based filtering (which post-filters results), parameter-based filtering **re-executes the SQL query** with the new parameter value, pushing the filter down to the data source.

**How it works:**
1. The dataset SQL uses `WHERE array_contains(:benefit_category, clm_dtl_benefit_category)`
2. The **default value** (`Medical`) loads on dashboard open — no user action needed
3. The filter dropdown below (populated from a lookup dataset) lets users **change the selection at any time**
4. Changing the filter **re-runs the query** with the new parameter values, updating all widgets below

frame.title: Total Paid by Place of Service (Filtered)
